my_thoughts: 'You were dismissive'—said it. From hinting (T1) to denying (T2) to naming (T3). Teacher accepts feedback without defending. 'Thank you' validates honesty. This teaches: criticism allowed, even helpful.
accumulated_insight: T3: Student admits 'Well, I thought you were dismissive earlier.' Named criticism. Teacher validates: 'Thank you for telling me.' Accepts feedback. Predicting surface_emotion as honesty is validated.
visible_response:
There’s a thought in your head that I think badly of you, but there is no evidence to support the thought in your head.
Could this be your critical conscience inside you calling you names?
